title: Harry Potter — The Descent and Return of the Chosen Soul
subtitle: Horcruxes as corrupted cakras, the Deathly Hallows as the three gunas, and the unus-ambo realised in the final battle

summary.one_line: Rowling's seven-volume epic mapped onto the Tantric-Śaiva architecture of the soul: the 7 Horcruxes as the 7 cakras corrupted, the Deathly Hallows as the three gunas, Voldemort's soul-splitting as ...
summary.scope: Covers all seven Harry Potter novels, with primary focus on the esoteric architecture underlying the plot: the Horcrux system, the Deathly Hallows, the Mirror of Erised, Dumbledore as vidyeśvara/guru, and the threshold dynamics of the Resurrection Stone.

sources.label: Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (1997)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (1998)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban (1999)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (2000)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix (2003)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2005)
Rowling, J.K. — Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows (2007)

body.text: [See HP Half-Blood Prince ch.23 and Deathly Hallows throughout. Voldemort creates 7 Horcruxes — 7 intentional fissures in the soul-corpus that correspond inversely to the 7 cakras. Where the cakras are wheels of energy-transformation ascending the subtle body, the Horcruxes are wheels of energy-fixation descending. Each requires a murder (the extreme contraction of I-consciousness into the other-as-object) and a ritual of binding. The 7-fold structure mirrors the 7 cakras of the Tantric subtle body: the corruption begins at the base (the diary/Gaunt ring = mūlādhāra) and culminates at the crown (Harry himself as the unintended 7th = sahasrāra). Voldemort's soul does not ascend; it descends into fragmentation.]

[See HP Deathly Hallows ch.21-22. The three Deathly Hallows correspond to the three guṇas of Sāṃkhya. The Elder Wand = rajas (passion, action, domination — the wand that wins every duel, the force of aggressive assertion). The Resurrection Stone = tamas (darkness, inertia, the past — the stone that calls forth the dead, the weight of what has been). The Invisibility Cloak = sattva (purity, clarity, transcendence — the cloak that hides from Death himself, the quality that sees through illusion). Harry, as the master of all three, transmutes the guṇas back into their source: he breaks the wand (dissolves rajas), drops the stone in the forest (dissolves tamas), and keeps the cloak (sattva integrated). This is the guṇa-ātita — the one beyond the guṇas.]

[See HP Deathly Hallows ch.34. The Resurrection Stone is the threshold device par excellence. When Harry activates it in the Forbidden Forest, the dead appear not as ghosts but as 'neither ghost nor spirit' — they are the daimonic presences, the denizens of the threshold realm. They walk beside him without touching the earth; they speak comfort without offering solutions; they prepare him for the crossing. The Stone is the instrument that opens the track between the worlds — the madhyama level where the daimon becomes visible. James, Lily, Sirius, and Lupin form a tetrad of protectors, each corresponding to an element of the soul's constitution: they are the perfected ancestors who escort the initiate to the sacrifice.]

[See HP Philosopher's Stone ch.12. The Mirror of Erised ('desire' spelled backward) shows 'not your face but your heart's desire.' It is the daimon's reflection — the image of the soul's own deepest longing that appears only when the soul is ready to see it. Dumbledore's gloss is crucial: 'It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.' The mirror is dangerous not because it lies but because it reveals too truly. Harry sees his family — the wholeness he lacks. This is the daimonic image: the soul's own completeness projected onto the threshold until the soul can internalise it. The mirror is the phantasia developed to the point where it becomes a window.]

[See HP Philosopher's Stone through Half-Blood Prince. Dumbledore is the vidyeśvara — the lord of knowledge who guides the soul through the stages of the path. He rarely gives direct answers; he gives hints, tasks, the apparatus of self-discovery. His death in Half-Blood Prince is not abandonment but the necessary withdrawal of the guru so the śiṣya (student) can stand alone — the exact structure of the vidyeśvara's grace: the master removes himself precisely when the student is ready. Dumbledore's posthumous guidance through the Pensieve memories is the transmission of śaktipāta (descent of grace) across the threshold of death itself.]

[See HP Deathly Hallows ch.36. The final duel in the Great Hall is the unus-ambo realised. Voldemort and Harry are not two separate beings who happen to fight — they are the same soul-continuum at two stages of the same process. Voldemort is what Harry would become if the contraction became total; Harry is what Voldemort was before the contraction began. The killing curse rebounding is not a magical trick but the structural necessity of nonduality: the same Self cannot destroy itself. When Voldemort falls, his body 'blank and expressionless' — the contraction undone, the I-consciousness released back into its source. The Elder Wand, the symbol of the separated will, refuses to harm its true master. Harry has become the unus-ambo: the one who knows there never were two.]

gaps: Full mapping of each Horcrux to a specific cakra with textual evidence needs elaboration
Each Horcrux destruction sequence could be individual passages
Deeper analysis of the Hallows-as-gunas in the Tale of the Three Brothers needed
King's Cross chapter as bardo vision could be its own passage

issues.title: All passages are meta-commentary — need primary text extraction
issues.description: Passages currently describe the esoteric mapping. Real passages should include actual quotations from the novels with analysis.

issues.title: Missing wandlore as subtle anatomy
issues.description: The deep wandlore of Ollivander — the wand choosing the wizard, the twin cores, the allegiance mechanics — maps directly onto the subtle body doctrine of the Tantras. Not yet integrated.

issues.title: Sorting Hat as karmic classification
issues.description: The Sorting Hat's four Houses correspond to the four faculties of the antahkarana (manas, buddhi, ahamkara, citta). Not yet developed.

versions.summary: Initial RO created mapping the Harry Potter series onto Tantric-Śaiva esoteric architecture. Six passages covering Horcruxes as cakras, Deathly Hallows as gunas, Resurrection Stone as threshold, Mirror of Erised as daimon, Dumbledore as guru, and the final battle as unus-ambo.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state with 7 primary sources and 6 passages
